_meta.probe: POST /v1/chat/completions, merge_intervals coding task, max_tokens=256, temp=0.3, after warmup (matches 3090 benchmarks/quality/specdec-v0512-2026-05-29.json for apples-to-apples)
_meta.purpose: head-to-head vs 3090's AWQ+draft results (their 306 coder-30b / 126 qwen36)
_meta.verdict: Absolute tok/s goes to the 3090 on both models, for structural reasons. R9700 wins the coder-30b speedup multiplier + acceptance (wide ladder its 32GB fits, the 3090's 24GB does not) and runs spec-decode at full context.

rtx3090.ladder: steps4/topk4/draft8 (wide ladder OOMs draft graphs on 24GB)

coder-30b.absolute_winner: 3090 (Marlin INT4 GEMM: ~8x our INT4 baseline; verify-step-bound, no draft closes it)

r9700.note: no spec-v2: SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2 needs --mamba-scheduler-strategy extra_buffer which AssertionErrors on RDNA4 (CUDA/MUSA/NPU only)

rtx3090.note: SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2=1 + extra_buffer lifts DFlash acceptance to 5.62

structural_gaps_on_rdna4: Marlin INT4 GEMM: 3090 INT4 decode baseline ~8x R9700 (185 vs 22 on coder-30b). Spec-decode amplifies a faster verify step; it cannot make the verify step itself faster.

SGLANG_ENABLE_SPEC_V2 + --mamba-scheduler-strategy extra_buffer: gated to CUDA/MUSA/NPU (asserts on ROCm). It lifts DFlash acceptance 3.75->5.62 on Qwen3_5MoeForConditionalGeneration; unavailable to us.

Where R9700 could win absolute: DeltaNet dense (Qwen3.5/3.6-27B) where our baseline already beats the 3090 ~2x (Triton wave32). But spec-decode there is blocked on both stacks pending a BF16-MTP recast (DFlash drafts OOM the DeltaNet KV).
note_on_our_prior_numbers: Our README spec-decode summary lists tuned/workload-favorable figures (e.g. REAM-A3B 133 tok/s was a thinking-workload median, accept 7.49). On this standard coding probe REAM-A3B+DFlash = 69.3 (accept 3.75) and coder-30b+EAGLE3 = 108 @16K (vs the table's 97 @256K). Numbers are context- and workload-dependent; compare like-for-like.
